response.justification: Bogdan is a skilled full-stack and low-latency web developer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed systems, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His current AI-adjacent work involves MCP tooling and agentic workflows at the application layer, not hardware or systems-level AI acceleration research.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby) shows performance-aware engineering mindset
Broad polyglot background including C/C++ and embedded work suggests adaptability across system layers
MIT CS degree provides a solid foundational qualification
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training and inference optimization
Career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack; no evidence of pivoting toward hardware-software co-design or AI accelerator research
